title: Vesta Refund-Cycle Anomaly with Clean Transaction Behavior Pattern
narrative: Fraudster exploits merchant refund policies by executing legitimate-appearing transactions followed by refunds, then immediately recharging the card with different merchants or MCCs. Raw behavioral signals remain within normal parameters — amounts are modest, velocity is controlled, device appears consistent. However, Vesta V-fields encode the refund-recharge cycle signature through transaction-sequence features that track refund timing, cross-merchant patterns, and charge-reversal-charge triplets. V307 captures the refund-then-charge sequence velocity while V280 flags the cross-MCC pattern anomaly. The fraud operates by gaming refund windows to extract value through gift cards or cryptocurrency purchases made immediately after securing refunds, creating a value-extraction loop invisible in single-transaction analysis.
indicators: V307 > 4.5 (p85), elevated refund-sequence timing feature indicating charge-refund-recharge cycle
V280 between 2.8 and 5.2, cross-MCC transaction pattern anomaly score
C13 > 45 (p75), card-level refund counter aggregation above typical cardholder baseline
V201 between 1.2 and 2.8, refund-window timing feature flagging rapid recharge pattern
card1_amt_zscore between -0.8 and +1.2, amount within normal cardholder spending range
indicator_explanations: Transaction-sequence timing suggests a charge followed by refund then immediate recharge pattern
Card has been used across multiple merchant categories in an unusual sequence
Pattern indicates transaction reversals followed by new charges within short timeframe
Card shows elevated refund activity compared to typical legitimate cardholders
Time between refund and subsequent charge is unusually short for legitimate behavior
Transaction amount appears normal for this cardholder masking the fraud operation
summary: Fraudster cycles through charge-refund-recharge sequences to extract value while keeping individual transactions appearing legitimate. Raw behavioral signals like amount and velocity remain clean, but Vesta engineered features detect the refund-exploitation pattern.
reasoning: Legitimate cardholders rarely execute rapid refund-recharge cycles across different merchant categories. The pattern indicates systematic exploitation of refund policies to extract value through secondary purchases like gift cards or cryptocurrency, with the refund-recharge timing signature captured in V-field sequence encodings invisible to standard fraud rules.
